message:
fix(indexer): bounded retention for .lexical-publish-backups/ [HIGH, 0k0sk]

Problem (bead 0k0sk): `publish_staged_lexical_index` at src/indexer/mod.rs
retained EVERY prior lexical index indefinitely under
`<data_dir>/.lexical-publish-backups/<unique-name>/`. `unique_lexical_publish_backup_path`
always produced a fresh path, and nothing ever read the backups dir to
prune. For real cass corpora (~50k docs, multi-hundred-MB Tantivy trees),
disk usage grew linearly and unboundedly across publishes — laptop/SSD
operators ran out of space within days of heavy indexing, breaking all
subsequent writes with "No space left" / "database is busy" errors.

The previous behavior was pinned by
`publish_staged_lexical_index_retains_every_backup_unboundedly_today_pending_retention_cap`
which asserted 4 retained backups after 4 publishes — explicitly as a
placeholder-until-retention-lands.

Fix:

1. Add `lexical_publish_backup_retention_limit()` — reads
   `CASS_LEXICAL_PUBLISH_BACKUP_RETENTION` env var, defaults to 1.
   0 = disable retention entirely; N = keep N most-recent.

2. Add `prune_lexical_publish_backups()` — lists the backups dir,
   sorts newest-first by mtime (with stable path-order tiebreaker
   for sub-nanosecond filesystems), keeps the first
   `retention_limit`, removes the rest via `fs::remove_dir_all`.
   Emits structured `tracing::info!` per pruned backup with
   `freed_bytes` + `retention_limit` + `backups_dir` fields so
   operators can correlate disk recovery to publishes.

3. Add `directory_size_bytes_best_effort()` — iterative recursive
   byte count for tracing only; never fails the caller.

4. Hook prune into `publish_staged_lexical_index` AFTER the
   successful publish + retain + fsync — pruning is cleanup
   hygiene, so a prune failure logs a warning but does NOT fail
   the publish (live index is already committed).

Tests:

- Updated `publish_staged_lexical_index_retains_every_backup_unboundedly_today_pending_retention_cap`
  (renamed to `_prunes_retained_backups_to_default_retention_cap`)
  to assert `retained_backups.len() == 1` after 4 publishes with
  the default cap. Marked `#[serial]` because it clears the env
  var. Restores prior env state at the end.

- Added `publish_staged_lexical_index_retention_cap_is_env_configurable`
  — exercises both `CASS_LEXICAL_PUBLISH_BACKUP_RETENTION=0`
  (expect 0 retained) and `=3` (expect 3 retained) after 5 publishes.
  Proves the env knob flows through to the prune step.

- Two existing serial tests
  (`_retains_stale_in_progress_backup_when_live_present` from b757d822
  and `_recovers_from_crash_between_linux_swap_and_retain` from 9wkx5)
  were asserting ==2 retained backups as a proxy for their
  recovery-semantics invariants. Both now set
  `CASS_LEXICAL_PUBLISH_BACKUP_RETENTION=2` inside the test (marked
  `#[serial]`, restore prior env at end) so the retention cap does
  not interfere with the recovery assertions they actually pin.

All 10 `publish_staged_lexical_index` unit tests pass.

Refs coding_agent_session_search-0k0sk

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
